chapters.chapter_summary: The patient was positioned supine with a shoulder roll elevating the right shoulder and head, exposing the temporal-parietal area. General anesthesia was administered.

A circular bone flap was elevated using a single burr hole, exposing the underlying relaxed dura. The surgical corridor was optimized for a dissection plane parallel to the primary motor and sensory cortex.

The dura mater was opened, and the tumor was identified and distinguished from the normal brain. Sodium fluorescein-guided microscopy combined with computer navigation was used to confirm the location of the tumor. The tumor was carefully dissected from the surrounding brain tissue.

The tumor was removed in a gross total fashion. Sodium fluorescein-guided microscopy was used to identify any residual tumor in the inferior border of the exposure. Additional tumor was discovered and removed.

Hydrogen peroxide-soaked cotton balls were applied over the resection cavity for hemostasis and destruction of neoplastic cells. The cavity was covered with a hemostatic agent, and the dura was re-approximated.
The bone flap was re-approximated and secured with titanium microplates. The border between the bone flap and the cranium was covered with a synthetic agent for a better cosmetic result.
The patient had no neurological deficit and was discharged home. Postoperative MRI scans showed no radiographic evidence of residual or recurrent tumor.

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a brain tumor resection on a 20-plus year old patient who presented with headaches, progressive weakness on the left side of the body, and difficulty with gait and balance. The patient was alert and oriented but had left-sided hemiparesis with upper tract signs.
SOAP.Objective: I identified a large mass at the temporoparietal junction with significant local mass effect and vasogenic edema. I observed the tumor's location using sodium fluorescein-guided microscopy and computer navigation. I performed the resection using a dissection plane parallel to the primary motor and sensory cortex.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful with gross total resection of the tumor. I identified and removed additional residual tumor using sodium fluorescein-guided microscopy. Postoperative MRI scans showed no residual or recurrent tumor.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the wound in layers and secured the bone flap with titanium microplates. I covered the border between the bone flap and the cranium with a synthetic agent for a better cosmetic result. The patient was discharged home with no neurological deficit.
